data.content: Now I have a clear picture of the `OrderService` class. Let me create comprehensive MSTest tests.
data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. The user wants MSTest unit tests for the `OrderService` class. Let me also check the legacy antipatterns to understand what to avoid, and the existing modern-mstest fixture to see if there are already test files.

Let me look at the legacy antipatterns for context on what NOT to do:

data.content: Good — I can see the anti-patterns to avoid. Now let me create the test file:

toolCalls.name: create

data.content: I have a duplicate helper method and an unused field. Let me fix that:

arguments.new_str:
[TestClass]
public sealed class OrderServiceTests
{
    private static List<OrderItem> CreateItems(params (string Name, decimal Price, int Qty)[] items) =>
        items.Select(i => new OrderItem { ProductName = i.Name, Price = i.Price, Quantity = i.Qty }).ToList();

    [TestClass]
    public sealed class CreateOrder

data.reasoningText:
Wait, I have the `CreateItems` helper method defined both at the outer class level and in the nested classes need it. But since the nested classes are inside `OrderServiceTests`, they can access the static method from the parent class. However, I duplicated the method - I have it defined at line 11 and again at the bottom. Let me fix this. Actually looking more carefully, I have the method defined at line 11 (inside `OrderServiceTests` before the nested classes) and then again at the bottom. That's a compilation error - duplicate member. Let me also reconsider - the nested classes should be able to access the outer class's static method.


I also notice the `_service` field in the outer class is unused since each nested class instantiates its own, so I should remove that too.

arguments.new_str:

        items.Select(i => new OrderItem { ProductName = i.Name, Price = i.Price, Quantity = i.Qty }).ToList();

    [TestClass]
    public sealed class CreateOrder

The project is missing `ImplicitUsings` — it doesn't have the global using for `System`, `System.Collections.Generic`, etc. I need to check the project file. The project file doesn't have `<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>`. Let me enable that or add the necessary using directives.

Actually, since I shouldn't modify the existing project setup, let me just add the correct `using` directives to the test file. But the OrderService.cs also has issues — it's missing `using System;` and `using System.Collections.Generic;` etc. Since the project belongs to this fixture and we're supposed to make it all work, let me enable ImplicitUsings in the project file.
